health, and can again stand among my comrads and in a few days I hope that I shall again feel as well as before,
you wrote that it was lonesome times in old grove and I should think so to for though far away I can well imagin how lonesome it must seem. you wrote that - alvira and welington was dead, and william and welthy. [soon] I should think by the way you wrote was sick and Harlem was sick at geneseo and father had gone to see him. I have herd since that he had gone to his long and eternal home. oh, how unexpected was this news to me and how it rung my soul with anguish, when I thought that I was the most in danger my self. those, that I least expected it were cut down like a flower in autumn. this is the first thing that ever overwhelmed me it is the first time that I ever knew real sarrow and it is the first time that I ever felt sick at hart. and god knows that I hope it will be the last.

you wrote that you should like to know my true circumstances I do not know that I can explain all of them to you, but I will try, our we live in tents mede of cloth, our beds are straw and cedar brush and it makes a very good bed for a soldier, our living is as good as could be expected. it consists princeiply of bread meat rice beens sugar molasses coffee and tea, think not that we suffer here on the acount of some thing to eat for we fare well for soldiers, but we should not have
